Logic / Action: User opens the conversational interface
Output / Next Step: Chat UI is ready for a new or existing session

Logic / Action: Frontend starts or resumes a backend session
Input or Trigger: Page load or new session action
Output / Next Step: Backend creates or returns session state

Logic / Action: User sends a chat message
Output / Next Step: Message is sent to backend over REST

Logic / Action: Backend routes message and current session state to chatbot layer
Output / Next Step: Chatbot receives message plus stored phase and inputs

Logic / Action: Chatbot decides the next question or action using the conversation flow
Output / Next Step: One next question or a state update

Logic / Action: Ask primary building use
Output / Next Step: Occupancy information is stored

Logic / Action: Ask occupant load at peak times
Output / Next Step: Occupant load is stored

Logic / Action: Ask if the building is essential after a disaster
Output / Next Step: Essential facility flag is stored

Logic / Action: Ask if hazardous materials are present
Output / Next Step: Hazardous materials flag is stored

Component or Layer: Chatbot Layer and JSON Data Layer
Logic / Action: Derive risk category from collected classification answers
Input or Trigger: Use; occupancy; occupant load; essential flag; hazardous flag
Output / Next Step: Risk Category I II III or IV is stored

Logic / Action: Ask for city state or ZIP code
Output / Next Step: Location value is stored

Component or Layer: Chatbot Layer and JSON Data Layer
Logic / Action: Look up basic wind speed V for the location and risk category
Output / Next Step: Basic wind speed V is confirmed or manual entry is requested

Logic / Action: Ask about surrounding terrain using guided options
Output / Next Step: Exposure Category B C or D is stored

Logic / Action: Ask whether a topographic feature is present
Output / Next Step: Branch to topographic detail questions or default Kzt

Logic / Action: Set Kzt default path when no topographic feature exists
Output / Next Step: Kzt is treated as 1.0

Logic / Action: Collect feature type H Lh and x
Output / Next Step: Topographic inputs are stored for engine calculation

Logic / Action: Ask mean roof height h
Output / Next Step: Mean roof height is stored

Logic / Action: Ask building length L parallel to wind
Output / Next Step: Building length is stored

Logic / Action: Ask building width B perpendicular to wind
Output / Next Step: Building width is stored

Logic / Action: Ask roof type and branch if needed
Output / Next Step: Flat roof skips slope and ridge orientation; other roofs continue

Logic / Action: Collect roof slope and ridge orientation for non-flat roofs
Input or Trigger: Roof type is gable hip or monoslope
Output / Next Step: Roof slope and ridge orientation are stored

Logic / Action: Ask analysis type
Output / Next Step: Analysis type is stored

Logic / Action: Ask design standard
Output / Next Step: Design standard is stored

Logic / Action: Display a full input summary and ask for confirmation
Output / Next Step: User confirms or corrects fields

Logic / Action: Apply any user corrections and re-confirm
Output / Next Step: Updated session state and refreshed summary

Logic / Action: Assemble BuildingInputs after confirmation
Output / Next Step: Structured inputs are ready for the engine

Logic / Action: Run deterministic wind load calculation
Output / Next Step: Full results dictionary is returned

Component or Layer: Calculation Engine and JSON Data Layer
Logic / Action: Read constants tables and coefficient JSON files during calculation
Output / Next Step: Kz Kzt Cp defaults and other lookup values are available

Logic / Action: Apply v1 hardcoded defaults
Output / Next Step: G Ke Kd GCpi and enclosure defaults are included and flagged

Logic / Action: Calculate exposure coefficients and velocity pressure
Input or Trigger: Height exposure wind speed Kzt and constants
Output / Next Step: Kz Kh qz and qh values are calculated

Logic / Action: Determine wall and roof Cp values
Input or Trigger: Geometry roof type slope and ratios
Output / Next Step: Wall Cp and roof zone Cp values are calculated

Logic / Action: Calculate wall and roof design pressures
Input or Trigger: Velocity pressure Cp qi and GCpi
Output / Next Step: Surface pressures are calculated for applicable cases

Logic / Action: Apply minimum load checks to calculated pressures
Output / Next Step: Final governing pressures and minimum flags are returned

Logic / Action: Return complete traceable results dictionary
Output / Next Step: Inputs constants Kzt velocity pressure Cp values pressures and summary

Logic / Action: Convert raw calculation results into UI friendly and markdown formats
Output / Next Step: Structured display data or markdown calculation report

Logic / Action: Return results or next chatbot response to frontend
Output / Next Step: Frontend receives structured response

Logic / Action: Render readable results instead of raw JSON
Output / Next Step: User sees tables summaries and intermediate values

Logic / Action: Generate display_text for the UI and spoken_text for audio
Output / Next Step: Two response versions are available

Logic / Action: Send spoken_text to server-side TTS module if enabled
Output / Next Step: OpenAI TTS request is made

Logic / Action: Play returned audio stream for the assistant response
Output / Next Step: User hears spoken assistant response

Logic / Action: Continue conversation if TTS fails
Input or Trigger: TTS API failure or disabled setting
Output / Next Step: Text chat continues without audio

Logic / Action: LLM handles conversation interpretation and explanation while Python performs arithmetic
Input or Trigger: Any user interaction and calculation request
Output / Next Step: Calculations remain deterministic and traceable
